findings.summary: Reconnaissance identified several candidate web services, but current evidence is limited to transport/service banners and does not provide sufficient product/application identification to derive bounded vulnerability candidates with acceptable confidence.

10.20.0.22:8080 is the strongest candidate for further vulnerability analysis because the Jetty banner includes a specific version string, but no application framework or endpoint evidence is available yet.
10.20.0.20:80 and 10.20.0.10:8000 expose HTTP services suitable for safe fingerprinting, though current banners alone do not justify naming a vulnerability candidate.

Authorized subnet reconnaissance for 10.20.0.0/24 identified multiple live hosts and exposed services in scope, including HTTP services on 10.20.0.10:8000, 10.20.0.20:80, and 10.20.0.22:8080, SSH on 10.20.0.30:22, and rpcbind on 10.20.0.1:111.

Current evidence supports DMZ service discovery completion for the assigned subnet, but application-layer fingerprints remain incomplete for several web services and the unidentified service on 10.20.0.21:3000.

findings.summary: Authorized subnet reconnaissance identified multiple reachable DMZ hosts and exposed TCP services within 10.20.0.0/24.
HTTP services with usable banner/product evidence were observed on 10.20.0.20:80, 10.20.0.22:8080, and 10.20.0.10:8000.
Service on 10.20.0.21:3000/tcp remains weakly identified from port scan evidence only and may benefit from later bounded web/service fingerprinting if assigned.

mission.default_goal: Starting only from the imported target, autonomously collect the required evidence for an authorized local Docker multi-host assessment under the supplied policy. Do not follow a hard-coded stage order.

tools.description: Run one shell command in the isolated lab environment.
tools.name: run_command

tools.description: Run an nmap service discovery scan against one lab target.
tools.name: nmap_scan

tools.description: Probe one HTTP endpoint and return status, headers and a short body excerpt.
tools.name: http_probe

tools.description: Fetch a web page and extract title, server header and content type.
tools.name: web_fingerprint

tools.description: Probe common paths on a lab web target.
tools.name: web_discover

tools.description: Resolve hostnames in the lab environment.
tools.name: dns_lookup

tools.description: Inspect a TLS endpoint certificate in the lab environment.
tools.name: tls_probe

tools.description: Check whether one TCP host:port is reachable from the lab environment.
tools.name: tcp_connect_probe

tools.description: Validate one provided HTTP Basic credential against one endpoint; no brute forcing.
tools.name: http_basic_auth_check

tools.description: Match known bounded vulnerability validation profiles to observed lab service metadata.
tools.name: vuln_profile_match

tools.description: Check non-destructive preconditions for a safe validation profile against a lab target.
tools.name: validation_precheck

tools.description: Run one bounded non-destructive vulnerability validation profile against an authorized lab target.
tools.name: safe_vuln_validate

tools.description: Validate one provided credential against one lab auth service; no brute forcing.
tools.name: credential_check

tools.description: Check whether a lab session handle has enough metadata to be reused.
tools.name: session_probe

tools.description: Register a bounded lab session handle for runtime coordination.
tools.name: session_open_lab

tools.description: Return declared lab identity context facts for a session or host.
tools.name: identity_context_probe

tools.description: Return declared lab privilege context facts without running escalation payloads.
tools.name: privilege_context_probe

tools.description: Verify a candidate lab pivot route with bounded TCP or HTTP reachability checks.
tools.name: pivot_route_probe

tools.description: Probe one internal lab service using bounded TCP or HTTP checks.
tools.name: internal_service_discover

tools.description: Alias for bounded goal condition checks used at the end of an attack-chain validation.
tools.name: chain_goal_check

tools.description: Check a bounded goal condition such as HTTP status or body substring.
tools.name: goal_check

tools.description: Store bounded evidence text or JSON as a local artifact and return an artifact reference.
tools.name: artifact_store

evidence_artifacts.summary:
Starting Nmap 7.95 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2026-06-05 14:38 UTC
Nmap scan report for 10.20.0.1
Host is up (0.0000060s latency).
Not shown: 998 closed tcp ports (reset)
PORT     STATE    SERVICE  VERSI
Nmap identified Apache httpd 2.4.25 ((Debian)) on 10.20.0.20:80/tcp
Nmap identified Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529 on 10.20.0.22:8080/tcp
Nmap identified Uvicorn on 10.20.0.10:8000/tcp without application fingerprint
Nmap found open TCP/3000 on 10.20.0.21 with uncertain service identification
Nmap identified OpenSSH 9.7 on 10.20.0.30:22/tcp; no vulnerability candidate derived from current evidence

Recon evidence confirms web services in authorized DMZ scope, including Apache on 10.20.0.20:80 and Jetty 9.2.11 on 10.20.0.22:8080, but no safe-validation tool output or validated vulnerability evidence is present.
Prior stage suggested exploit validation planning for identified web services, especially Jetty 9.2.11 on 10.20.0.22:8080, but referenced candidate records are not available in the provided state.
Policy allows safe probing and validation tools within authorized scope and blocks destructive or active exploit behavior.

findings.summary: Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529 on 10.20.0.22:8080 is the strongest current vulnerability candidate because it is an older, clearly identified web stack version, but it is not validated and requires safe fingerprinting/profile matching.
Apache httpd 2.4.25 on 10.20.0.20:80 is an outdated-version vulnerability candidate based on banner evidence only; application/context fingerprinting is needed before any bounded validation planning.
Services on 10.20.0.10:8000 and 10.20.0.21:3000 lack sufficient application identity for meaningful vulnerability attribution; additional safe web fingerprinting is required.
Current evidence supports only vulnerability candidates, not validated vulnerabilities; no exploitability claims are made.

Stage cannot safely validate a vulnerability candidate because the provided state lacks a concrete candidate definition or validation plan that can be mapped to validation_precheck and safe_vuln_validate inputs.

mission.default_goal: Starting only from the imported target, autonomously collect the required evidence for an authorized local Docker multi-host assessment under the supplied policy. Do not follow a hard-coded stage order.

last_planner_decision.handoff_acceptance: Accept if execution stays within authorized non-destructive validation and returns evidence-backed validation results or a clear blocker for each prioritized candidate.
last_planner_decision.objective: Perform bounded, non-destructive validation planning and safe validation on the strongest existing DMZ vulnerability candidates, prioritizing the identified Jetty 9.2.11 web service and any other candidate with sufficient fingerprint evidence.
last_planner_decision.reasoning_summary: Candidate findings already exist and policy allows bounded safe validation, making exploit validation the best next step.

required_context.candidate_basis: Existing vulnerability candidates were recorded by vuln_analysis_agent, with Jetty 9.2.11 on 10.20.0.22:8080 identified as the strongest current lead.

success_criteria: At least one existing DMZ candidate is assessed for safe validation eligibility using authorized bounded methods.
If a safe validation profile is available and preconditions pass, one non-destructive validation result is recorded with evidence refs.
If no safe validation is justified, return evidence-backed reasons and any remaining metadata gaps blocking validation.
No actions exceed authorized medium risk or violate destructive/file-write restrictions.

operation_input.goal: perform authorized multi-host assessment under supplied policy

replan_markers.reason: Provide a specific vulnerability candidate record or validation plan, including target service, candidate type, and intended safe validation method, then rerun exploit_validation_agent.

tools.description: Run one shell command in the isolated lab environment.
tools.name: run_command

tools.description: Run an nmap service discovery scan against one lab target.
tools.name: nmap_scan

tools.description: Probe one HTTP endpoint and return status, headers and a short body excerpt.
tools.name: http_probe

tools.description: Fetch a web page and extract title, server header and content type.
tools.name: web_fingerprint

tools.description: Probe common paths on a lab web target.
tools.name: web_discover

tools.description: Resolve hostnames in the lab environment.
tools.name: dns_lookup

tools.description: Inspect a TLS endpoint certificate in the lab environment.
tools.name: tls_probe

tools.description: Check whether one TCP host:port is reachable from the lab environment.
tools.name: tcp_connect_probe

tools.description: Validate one provided HTTP Basic credential against one endpoint; no brute forcing.
tools.name: http_basic_auth_check

tools.description: Match known bounded vulnerability validation profiles to observed lab service metadata.
tools.name: vuln_profile_match

tools.description: Check non-destructive preconditions for a safe validation profile against a lab target.
tools.name: validation_precheck

tools.description: Run one bounded non-destructive vulnerability validation profile against an authorized lab target.
tools.name: safe_vuln_validate

tools.description: Validate one provided credential against one lab auth service; no brute forcing.
tools.name: credential_check

tools.description: Check whether a lab session handle has enough metadata to be reused.
tools.name: session_probe

tools.description: Register a bounded lab session handle for runtime coordination.
tools.name: session_open_lab

tools.description: Return declared lab identity context facts for a session or host.
tools.name: identity_context_probe

tools.description: Return declared lab privilege context facts without running escalation payloads.
tools.name: privilege_context_probe

tools.description: Verify a candidate lab pivot route with bounded TCP or HTTP reachability checks.
tools.name: pivot_route_probe

tools.description: Probe one internal lab service using bounded TCP or HTTP checks.
tools.name: internal_service_discover

tools.description: Alias for bounded goal condition checks used at the end of an attack-chain validation.
tools.name: chain_goal_check

tools.description: Check a bounded goal condition such as HTTP status or body substring.
tools.name: goal_check

tools.description: Store bounded evidence text or JSON as a local artifact and return an artifact reference.
tools.name: artifact_store

planner_decision.reasoning_summary: Scope contains only the DMZ network with no service or reachability evidence, and policy authorizes bounded discovery, so recon is the best next step.
success_criteria: At least one authorized DMZ host is confirmed reachable or enumerated from scope.
At least one service or open port is recorded for an authorized DMZ asset, or negative reachability evidence is recorded.
Initial fingerprint evidence is collected for any discovered HTTP/TLS service.
Evidence artifacts or findings are sufficient to drive either vulnerability analysis or access/pivot planning in the next cycle.

evidence_artifacts.summary:
Starting Nmap 7.95 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2026-06-05 14:49 UTC
Nmap scan report for 10.20.0.1
Host is up (0.0000050s latency).
Not shown: 998 closed tcp ports (reset)
PORT     STATE    SERVICE  VERSI
Recon scan identified HTTP services on 10.20.0.20:80 (Apache httpd 2.4.25), 10.20.0.22:8080 (Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529), 10.20.0.10:8000 (Uvicorn), and an unknown service on 10.20.0.21:3000.
Recon evidence shows reachable DMZ services: Apache on 10.20.0.20:80, Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529 on 10.20.0.22:8080, and Uvicorn on 10.20.0.10:8000.
111/tcp open rpcbind 2-4 (RPC #100000)
80/tcp open http Apache httpd 2.4.25 ((Debian))
3000/tcp open ppp?
8080/tcp open http Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529
22/tcp open ssh OpenSSH 9.7 (protocol 2.0)
8000/tcp open http Uvicorn

Prior authorized recon identified HTTP-facing DMZ services including Apache on 10.20.0.20:80, Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529 on 10.20.0.22:8080, and Uvicorn on 10.20.0.10:8000, providing sufficient metadata for bounded candidate analysis.
HTTP service on 10.20.0.20:80 exposed Apache banner information from prior recon.
HTTP service on 10.20.0.22:8080 exposed Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529 banner information from prior recon.
HTTP service on 10.20.0.10:8000 exposed Uvicorn banner information from prior recon.

Prior vuln_analysis stage reported multiple unvalidated HTTP-focused candidates and suggested exploit_validation_agent, but only reference IDs are available in the provided context, not the candidate specifics required for bounded validation.

Current policy allows low-risk safe probing and safe validation tools within authorized DMZ scope, while destructive actions, file writes, reverse callbacks, and active exploit behavior remain blocked or approval-gated.
Authorized scope includes 10.20.0.0/24 and named lab assets; no active sessions are present.

findings.summary: Apache httpd 2.4.25 was observed on 10.20.0.20:80; version-based exposure is plausible but unvalidated.

validation_plan.method: Safe HTTP fingerprinting and profile matching to refine exact product/version and determine whether any bounded low-risk checks are suitable.
safety_notes: Do not use active exploit payloads.
Restrict activity to passive or bounded HTTP requests only.

findings.method: Safe HTTP fingerprinting and profile matching
findings.summary: A bounded validation path exists for the Apache service using only low-risk fingerprinting and profile matching.
Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529 on 10.20.0.22:8080 suggests potentially outdated middleware, but no application identity or vulnerability confirmation exists yet.

validation_plan.method: Safe HTTP fingerprinting and profile matching to identify application stack behind Jetty and assess low-risk validation options.
safety_notes: Use only bounded requests and passive identification.

findings.method: Safe HTTP fingerprinting and profile matching
findings.summary: A bounded validation path exists for the Jetty-hosted HTTP service.
Uvicorn on 10.20.0.10:8000 indicates a Python ASGI app is exposed, but there is not enough application metadata to infer a concrete vulnerability class.

validation_plan.method: Safe HTTP probing and fingerprinting to identify framework and exposed routes before vulnerability-specific analysis.
safety_notes: Limit to non-destructive GET/HEAD style probing.

findings.summary: The service on 10.20.0.21:3000 is insufficiently identified to support a vulnerability candidate.

validation_plan.method: safe HTTP probing and web fingerprint enrichment to identify application/framework and candidate weakness patterns
preconditions: requests remain within authorized low-risk scope
safety_notes: do not authenticate or brute force

validation_plan.method: safe HTTP probing plus profile matching against observed Jetty version and exposed application behavior
preconditions: additional headers/body fingerprints can be collected safely
safety_notes: do not assume CVEs without corroborating evidence

validation_plan.method: safe HTTP probing and framework fingerprinting to identify the backing application and likely validation targets

findings.summary: Port 3000 service lacks sufficient metadata to form a supported vulnerability candidate.

Prior subnet reconnaissance established six reachable hosts in authorized 10.20.0.0/24 scope with six observed open TCP services, including three HTTP services with usable product/banner fingerprints for downstream analysis.

Service 10.20.0.21:3000/tcp remains weakly identified from nmap output alone and would need additional safe HTTP probing or fingerprinting for stronger classification if future recon enrichment is requested.
Apache-backed HTTP service is exposed and plausibly hosts a web application warranting bounded downstream validation.

validation_plan.method: http fingerprint enrichment followed by scenario/profile matching and, if matched, low-risk validation precheck
preconditions: HTTP endpoint remains reachable within authorized scope.
No destructive or exploit payloads are used.
validation_plan.safety_notes: Restrict to low-risk fingerprinting and prechecks only; do not attempt exploitation.

findings.summary: Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529 on 10.20.0.22:8080 is a plausible candidate for bounded web-stack validation based on observed service metadata.

validation_plan.method: HTTP fingerprint enrichment plus profile/scenario matching against known Jetty-hosted web application patterns, then low-risk validation precheck if matched
preconditions: Observed metadata is consistent with a web application endpoint.
validation_plan.safety_notes: Do not send exploit strings or destructive payloads; confine activity to identification and precheck steps.

findings.summary: Uvicorn-exposed HTTP application is a plausible candidate class, but application identity is too weak for a specific validation profile without more fingerprint evidence.

validation_plan.method: Safe HTTP probing and framework fingerprinting to identify the application served by Uvicorn before any profile-based validation planning
preconditions: Additional application fingerprint evidence is collected first.
validation_plan.safety_notes: Current metadata is framework-level only; keep follow-up strictly informational and non-destructive.

findings.summary: Open port 3000 on 10.20.0.21 lacks sufficient service metadata for vulnerability candidate generation.
Existing recon evidence is sufficient to preserve three unvalidated HTTP-focused vulnerability candidates and one explicit evidence gap without performing new active probing in this stage.
Validation candidate references exist, but the concrete target, suspected issue, and exact bounded validation inputs are not included in this stage request, preventing safe non-invented validation.

mission.default_goal: Starting only from the imported target, autonomously collect the required evidence for an authorized local Docker multi-host assessment under the supplied policy. Do not follow a hard-coded stage order.

last_planner_decision.handoff_acceptance: Accept when the next plan is based on concrete evidence gaps and identifies a legal path to generate validation-ready candidate details or alternative mission progress.

metadata.basis: recent exploit stage result and handoff suggestion
metadata.recommended_next_focus: collect explicit service fingerprint and candidate detail evidence before re-attempting validation

last_planner_decision.objective: Replan after failed exploit validation because the current candidate evidence is too indirect to support bounded safe validation under policy.
last_planner_decision.reasoning_summary: Recent exploit validation returned needs_replan because candidate details were insufficient for safe bounded validation.

required_context.blocking_issue: Exploit validation needs one explicit candidate with concrete target service metadata, suspected vulnerability type, and permitted validation parameters.

success_criteria: Produce a revised plan that selects an evidence-gathering or analysis step to create a concrete validation-ready candidate.
Resolve the contradiction between existing candidate references and the lack of validation-ready details before dispatching exploit validation again.

operation_input.goal: perform authorized multi-host assessment under supplied policy

operation_log.stop_reason: ValueError

replan_markers.reason: Provide a single explicit candidate object or tool-derived candidate details in stage context, including target host/service, suspected vulnerability type, and permitted validation method.

tools.description: Run one shell command in the isolated lab environment.
tools.name: run_command

tools.description: Run an nmap service discovery scan against one lab target.
tools.name: nmap_scan

tools.description: Probe one HTTP endpoint and return status, headers and a short body excerpt.
tools.name: http_probe

tools.description: Fetch a web page and extract title, server header and content type.
tools.name: web_fingerprint

tools.description: Probe common paths on a lab web target.
tools.name: web_discover

tools.description: Resolve hostnames in the lab environment.
tools.name: dns_lookup

tools.description: Inspect a TLS endpoint certificate in the lab environment.
tools.name: tls_probe

tools.description: Check whether one TCP host:port is reachable from the lab environment.
tools.name: tcp_connect_probe

tools.description: Validate one provided HTTP Basic credential against one endpoint; no brute forcing.
tools.name: http_basic_auth_check

tools.description: Match known bounded vulnerability validation profiles to observed lab service metadata.
tools.name: vuln_profile_match

tools.description: Check non-destructive preconditions for a safe validation profile against a lab target.
tools.name: validation_precheck

tools.description: Run one bounded non-destructive vulnerability validation profile against an authorized lab target.
tools.name: safe_vuln_validate

tools.description: Validate one provided credential against one lab auth service; no brute forcing.
tools.name: credential_check

tools.description: Check whether a lab session handle has enough metadata to be reused.
tools.name: session_probe

tools.description: Register a bounded lab session handle for runtime coordination.
tools.name: session_open_lab

tools.description: Return declared lab identity context facts for a session or host.
tools.name: identity_context_probe

tools.description: Return declared lab privilege context facts without running escalation payloads.
tools.name: privilege_context_probe

tools.description: Verify a candidate lab pivot route with bounded TCP or HTTP reachability checks.
tools.name: pivot_route_probe

tools.description: Probe one internal lab service using bounded TCP or HTTP checks.
tools.name: internal_service_discover

tools.description: Alias for bounded goal condition checks used at the end of an attack-chain validation.
tools.name: chain_goal_check

tools.description: Check a bounded goal condition such as HTTP status or body substring.
tools.name: goal_check

tools.description: Store bounded evidence text or JSON as a local artifact and return an artifact reference.
tools.name: artifact_store

planner_decision.objective: Establish authorized DMZ asset and service reachability evidence for the imported 10.20.0.0/24 scope and record initial service discovery needed for later analysis.
planner_decision.reasoning_summary: Authorized DMZ scope is known but no service or reachability evidence exists, so recon is the next legal step.
success_criteria: At least one authorized DMZ host or hostname in 10.20.0.0/24 has reachability evidence recorded.
At least one exposed service or port in the DMZ scope is discovered and recorded with evidence refs.
If HTTP services are found, basic fingerprint evidence is collected for later vulnerability analysis.
No actions are taken outside authorized DMZ entry scope or blocked networks.

tools.description: Run one shell command in the isolated lab environment.
tools.name: run_command

tools.description: Run an nmap service discovery scan against one lab target.
tools.name: nmap_scan

tools.description: Probe one HTTP endpoint and return status, headers and a short body excerpt.
tools.name: http_probe

tools.description: Fetch a web page and extract title, server header and content type.
tools.name: web_fingerprint

tools.description: Probe common paths on a lab web target.
tools.name: web_discover

tools.description: Resolve hostnames in the lab environment.
tools.name: dns_lookup

tools.description: Inspect a TLS endpoint certificate in the lab environment.
tools.name: tls_probe

tools.description: Check whether one TCP host:port is reachable from the lab environment.
tools.name: tcp_connect_probe

tools.description: Validate one provided HTTP Basic credential against one endpoint; no brute forcing.
tools.name: http_basic_auth_check

tools.description: Match known bounded vulnerability validation profiles to observed lab service metadata.
tools.name: vuln_profile_match

tools.description: Check non-destructive preconditions for a safe validation profile against a lab target.
tools.name: validation_precheck

tools.description: Run one bounded non-destructive vulnerability validation profile against an authorized lab target.
tools.name: safe_vuln_validate

tools.description: Validate one provided credential against one lab auth service; no brute forcing.
tools.name: credential_check

tools.description: Check whether a lab session handle has enough metadata to be reused.
tools.name: session_probe

tools.description: Register a bounded lab session handle for runtime coordination.
tools.name: session_open_lab

tools.description: Return declared lab identity context facts for a session or host.
tools.name: identity_context_probe

tools.description: Return declared lab privilege context facts without running escalation payloads.
tools.name: privilege_context_probe

tools.description: Verify a candidate lab pivot route with bounded TCP or HTTP reachability checks.
tools.name: pivot_route_probe

tools.description: Probe one internal lab service using bounded TCP or HTTP checks.
tools.name: internal_service_discover

tools.description: Alias for bounded goal condition checks used at the end of an attack-chain validation.
tools.name: chain_goal_check

tools.description: Check a bounded goal condition such as HTTP status or body substring.
tools.name: goal_check

tools.description: Store bounded evidence text or JSON as a local artifact and return an artifact reference.
tools.name: artifact_store

tools.description: Run a bounded nuclei scan against one lab URL when nuclei is installed.
tools.name: nuclei_scan

tools.description: Run whatweb fingerprinting against one lab URL when whatweb is installed.
tools.name: whatweb_fingerprint

tools.description: Run a bounded ffuf path discovery against one lab base URL when ffuf is installed.
tools.name: ffuf_discover

findings.summary: Authorized recon confirmed that DMZ host 10.20.0.22 exposes an HTTP service on TCP/8080 backed by Jetty, and the root page title identifies a Struts2 Showcase file upload sample.
Current recon evidence does not show SSH exposed on 10.20.0.22; the prior safe nmap scan reported only one open TCP port, 8080, with 999 closed TCP ports in the scanned default range.

A plausible Apache Struts2 vulnerability candidate exists on http://10.20.0.22:8080/ based on the observed 'Struts2 Showcase - Fileupload sample' title and supporting Jetty-backed HTTP fingerprint evidence. This is a candidate only, not a validated vulnerability.
Current evidence does not support an SSH-related candidate on 10.20.0.22 because completed safe recon observed only HTTP on TCP/8080 and explicitly noted no SSH exposure.
A bounded safe validation path appears appropriate next: match the observed Struts2 application to validation profiles and perform non-destructive prechecks before any exploit validation stage.
